sketch2.title: Practice the core idea of OFFSET Clause

concept_overview.title: What is OFFSET Clause?
concept_overview.bodyMarkdown:
OFFSET Clause focuses on this idea: Skipping rows in results.

A good starting point is to say the requirement in plain language before writing SQL.

### Learning goals
- use OFFSET for pagination
- Practice the core idea of OFFSET Clause

goal_one.title: use OFFSET for pagination
goal_one.bodyMarkdown:
use OFFSET for pagination.

When practicing OFFSET Clause, start with a focused example and verify the result carefully.

goal_two.title: Practice the core idea of OFFSET Clause
goal_two.bodyMarkdown:
Practice the core idea of OFFSET Clause.

Use the topic idea to explain what the query is doing in plain English.

practice_note.title: How to practice OFFSET Clause
practice_note.bodyMarkdown:
Read the requirement, predict the result, run the query, and verify whether the output really answers the question.

That habit is especially important for OFFSET Clause.

module3_offset_clause_practice_sql.title: Practice: OFFSET Clause
module3_offset_clause_practice_sql.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows product names where the name contains the letter `a`.

Then run it.

help.hint_1: Put the letter inside a pattern like %a%

module3_offset_clause_practice_sql.starterCode:
-- Match text patterns
SELECT name
FROM products
WHERE name LIKE '%a%'

module3_offset_clause_core_concept.title: Core idea: OFFSET Clause
module3_offset_clause_core_concept.prompt: What is the best description of OFFSET Clause?

help.hint_1: Look for the option that matches the main concept directly.
help.hint_2: Ignore answers that describe unrelated SQL behavior.

module3_offset_clause_core_concept.hint: Match the answer to the topic summary.

options.a: It handles partial text matching, lists, ranges, and missing values.
options.b: It is only about table creation.
options.d: It permanently fixes bad data in storage.

module3_offset_clause_true_statements.title: True statements about OFFSET Clause
module3_offset_clause_true_statements.prompt: Select all true statements about OFFSET Clause.

help.hint_1: Pick statements that align with the topic summary and objectives.
help.hint_2: Reject statements that clearly describe unrelated SQL ideas.

module3_offset_clause_true_statements.hint: Use the summary and objectives as your guide.

options.a: LIKE can be used for pattern matching.
options.b: IS NULL checks for missing values.
options.d: = NULL is the standard way to test for nulls.
options.e: IN can be cleaner than many OR conditions.

module3_offset_clause_workflow_order.title: Workflow for OFFSET Clause
module3_offset_clause_workflow_order.prompt: Reorder the workflow ideas for OFFSET Clause.

help.concept: Choose the matching/filtering idea first, then write the condition, then verify the rows.
help.hint_1: Think about what should happen first.
help.hint_2: Then think about what should happen last.

tokens.t1: decide whether you are matching text, a list, a range, or nulls
tokens.t2: write the matching condition in WHERE
tokens.t3: run the query and verify the kept rows

module3_offset_clause_keyword_fill.title: Pattern matching keyword
module3_offset_clause_keyword_fill.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best SQL keyword.

help.hint_1: Think about which SQL term best completes the sentence.
help.hint_2: Use the topic meaning to eliminate unrelated options.

module3_offset_clause_keyword_fill.hint: Match the sentence to the right SQL term.
module3_offset_clause_keyword_fill.template: A common operator for basic text pattern matching is ____.
